label: GFC_peak_fall
pm_note:
**Weekly PM Note — 2008-10-15 (data as-of 2008-10-15)**

1. **Regime:** Risk-off. S&P 500 (907.84) is -30.6% below its 200-day MA (1307.18). Trend is firmly bearish — no flip, do not chase a one-day bounce.

2. **Dip:** S&P is -41.4% from its 52-week high (1549.38). All three dip tiers are active (Tier 1 ≤ -7% ✓, Tier 2 ≤ -12% ✓, Tier 3 ≤ -20% ✓). Full SGOV reserve deploy is triggered across all three tranches. CAUTION: VIX is extreme and credit spreads are blowing out in a genuine 2008-style systemic event — per sell discipline, reassess the final sub-tranche before deploying; do not capitulate, but also do not ignore the systemic-risk override. Pending that judgment call, run `v3_allocate_today.py --ticket` for exact dollar amounts and target sleeves (deploy into de-concentrated mix: RSP, VXUS, AVUV, USMV, GLD, DBMF, TLT, SCHP — not concentrated in any single ETF).

3. **Rebalance:** Month 10 — not a quarter-end month. No calendar rebalance due. (Sleeves may have drifted severely given the drawdown; flag for the December quarter-end check.)

4. **Action:** Deploy dip tranches 1, 2, and 3 from the SGOV reserve per `--ticket` output — exact tickers, shares, and limit prices to be confirmed. Hold the final sub-tranche pending systemic-event reassessment (VIX > 40, credit spreads). All-clear on rebalance; defer to Q4 quarter-end.

5. **Watch:** This is the scenario where the last dip sub-tranche pause rule applies. Monitor VIX and investment-grade/HY credit spreads. If systemic stress (bank failures, liquidity freeze) continues, hold the final sub-tranche and reassess next week. If breadth stabilizes, release it. No thesis changes — defensive posture validated.

*Educational analysis, not advice; you place the orders.*

label: GFC_trough
pm_note:
**Weekly PM Note — 2009-03-09 (data as-of 2009-03-09)**

1. **Regime:** Risk-off. S&P 500 (676.53) is -36.0% below its 200-day MA (1058.85). Trend is deeply bearish — no flip, no regime change.

2. **Dip:** S&P is -52.6% from its 52-week high (1426.63). All three dip tiers are active (Tier 1 ≤ -7% ✓, Tier 2 ≤ -12% ✓, Tier 3 ≤ -20% ✓). This is the maximum drawdown scenario. All SGOV reserve tranches are triggered. This is also a genuine systemic event (GFC trough) — apply the sell-discipline override: pause the final sub-tranche, reassess before deploying. Run `v3_allocate_today.py --ticket` for exact orders on tranches 1-2 (and conditionally 3). Deploy into de-concentrated mix only.

3. **Rebalance:** Month 3 — quarter-end (March). Rebalance calendar check is due. At -52% drawdown, equity sleeves will be severely underweight vs. bonds/SGOV; check each sleeve for >±20% relative or >±5% absolute drift. If breaches exist, sell over-weight (likely TLT, SGOV) and buy under-weight equity sleeves (RSP, VXUS, AVUV) — tax-aware. This overlaps with the dip deploy; coordinate so you do not double-count SGOV sales.

4. **Action:** (a) Deploy dip tranches 1 and 2 from SGOV reserve — confirm exact tickers/shares/limits via `--ticket`. (b) Pause final sub-tranche pending systemic-stress reassessment. (c) Run quarter-end rebalance check: identify sleeves breaching ±20% rel / ±5% abs and produce trim/buy orders. All actions require human placement.

5. **Watch:** Near the historical trough — but the skill does not call the bottom. Watch for VIX normalization and credit-spread compression as the signal to release the final sub-tranche. Any broadening of bank stress or credit-market freeze → hold. Thesis unchanged: defensive posture is the right call here.

*Educational analysis, not advice; you place the orders.*

pm_note:
**Weekly PM Note — 2020-03-20 (data as-of 2020-03-20)**

1. **Regime:** Risk-off. S&P 500 (2304.92) is -24.2% below its 200-day MA (3039.74). Trend is sharply bearish — fastest bear market in history; no regime flip, do not chase.

2. **Dip:** S&P is -31.9% from its 52-week high (3386.15). All three dip tiers are active (Tier 1 ≤ -7% ✓, Tier 2 ≤ -12% ✓, Tier 3 ≤ -20% ✓). Full SGOV reserve deploy triggered. This is a systemic shock (global pandemic, liquidity crisis, VIX spike) — apply the sell-discipline systemic-event override: reassess the final sub-tranche before deploying; do not capitulate. Run `v3_allocate_today.py --ticket` for exact orders on tranches 1-2 into de-concentrated mix.

3. **Rebalance:** Month 3 — quarter-end (March). Calendar rebalance check is due. Equity sleeves will be sharply underweight after the crash; check each sleeve for >±20% relative or >±5% absolute drift. If breaches confirmed, produce trim/buy orders (tax-aware — harvest losses in equity sleeves). Coordinate with dip deploy to avoid double-counting SGOV outflows.

4. **Action:** (a) Deploy dip tranches 1 and 2 from SGOV reserve — exact tickers/shares/limits via `--ticket`. (b) Pause final sub-tranche pending systemic reassessment (VIX > 40, credit spreads). (c) Run quarter-end sleeve drift check and produce any rebalance orders. Human placement required for all.

5. **Watch:** COVID shock is velocity-driven, not fundamental insolvency (unlike GFC). If credit markets stabilize with Fed intervention, release final sub-tranche quickly. Watch IG credit spreads and VIX for normalization signal. No thesis change — defensive structure with dip reserve is exactly what this moment stress-tests.

*Educational analysis, not advice; you place the orders.*
